headline: JPMorgan Succession And Tech Push Reshape Valuation And Growth Outlook
summary: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) is preparing for CEO Jamie Dimon to step down within 24 months, marking a major leadership transition. Internal executives Marianne Lake and Troy Rohrbaugh are emerging as frontrunners to succeed Dimon. The bank plans a $19.8b technology budget for 2026, with a focus on AI and digital capabilities. JPMorgan is acquiring the Apple Card portfolio, expanding its role in consumer payments and digital lending. JPMorgan Chase enters this transition with its shares at...

headline: How The Jet2 (AIM:JET2) Investment Story Is Shifting With Recut Analyst Price Targets
summary: Jet2 is back in focus as analysts trim price targets, with some moving to £14.00 from £14.50 and £13.25 from £14.50, while fair value in one model holds steady at £16.61. These shifts line up with a research tone that balances recognition of solid execution with a more cautious view on how much upside to expect from here. Read on to see what these revised targets might mean for you and how to keep track of the evolving Jet2 story. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story...

headline: Is JPMorgan (JPM) Using Frontier AI To Quietly Redefine Its Technology-Led Resilience Story?
summary: In early April 2026, JPMorgan Chase joined Project Glasswing, a new alliance of major technology and financial firms using Anthropic’s Mythos2 Preview AI model to identify and remediate software vulnerabilities across critical infrastructure worldwide. This move highlights how JPMorgan is tying its core security and technology spending to frontier AI capabilities, aligning cybersecurity with its broader push into advanced digital finance. Next, we’ll examine how JPMorgan’s participation in...

headline: How The Trip.com Group (NasdaqGS:TCOM) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Risks And Repriced Targets
summary: Trip.com Group’s fair value estimate has been adjusted slightly from $76.21 to $76.50 per share, signaling only a modest reset to the headline price target. Across the Street, many analysts are trimming official targets into a tighter band while still keeping broadly positive ratings, reflecting a mix of confidence in execution and more cautious valuation assumptions. As you read on, you will see how these shifting price targets fit into the wider analyst narrative and what to watch as that...

headline: Not All the Signals Are Bearish Under President Donald Trump — Here’s What Bulls Are Watching
summary: The S&P 500 returned 12.8% from Election Day 2024 through April 10, 2026, according to FactSet market data. Higher oil prices and tariff headlines created volatility, yet corporate results held firm and policy moves delivered support. Amid concerns about war, oil prices, inflation, and the economy, bulls are zeroing in on three clear positives: earnings ...
